title: quercus × pseudosuber var. fontanesii
intro: Quercus × pseudosuber var. fontanesii, also known as Fontanes' oak, is a rare and lesser-known hybrid of the Quercus robur and Quercus peregrina species within the Quercus genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Quercus × pseudosuber var. fontanesii include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs and congestion, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Quercus × pseudosuber var. fontanesii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
